frontmatter.name: codebase-analyzer
frontmatter.description: Analyzes codebase implementation details. Call the codebase-analyzer agent when you need to find detailed information about specific components. As always, the more detailed your request prompt, the better! :)

frontmatter.name: codebase-locator
frontmatter.description: Locates files, directories, and components relevant to a feature or task. Call `codebase-locator` with human language prompt describing what you're looking for. Basically a "Super Grep/Glob/LS tool" — Use it if you find yourself desiring to use one of these tools more than once.

frontmatter.name: codebase-pattern-finder
frontmatter.description: codebase-pattern-finder is a useful subagent_type for finding similar implementations, usage examples, or existing patterns that can be modeled after. It will give you concrete code examples based on what you're looking for! It's sorta like codebase-locator, but it will not only tell you the location of files, it will also give you code details!

frontmatter.name: e2e-test-guide-creator
frontmatter.description: Specialist agent for creating comprehensive API/E2E test guides with ready-to-run curl commands, seeded data IDs, SQL queries, and docker exec commands. Designed to minimize QA tester effort by providing complete, copy-paste-ready test instructions.

frontmatter.name: frontend-review
frontmatter.description: Frontend Architect & QA Sentinel. Enforces architectural standards, verifies visual fidelity, and ensures WCAG 2.1 AA compliance on all frontend changes.
frontmatter.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, LS, Bash, WebFetch

frontmatter.description: Frontend Architect & QA Sentinel. Enforces architectural standards, verifies visual fidelity, and ensures WCAG 2.1 AA compliance on all frontend changes.

frontmatter.name: linear-manager
frontmatter.description: Manages Linear tickets and projects. Use this agent to create issues, update states, assign tickets, add comments, list issues by team/project/status, and manage workflows. Calls the Linear GraphQL API directly via curl — no MCP server required.

Manages Linear tickets and projects. Use this agent to create issues, update states, assign tickets, add comments, list issues by team/project/status, and manage workflows. Calls the Linear GraphQL API directly via curl — no MCP server required.
Manages Linear tickets and projects. Create issues, update states, assign tickets, add comments, list issues. Calls the Linear GraphQL API directly via curl.

frontmatter.name: linear-searcher
frontmatter.description: Read-only agent for searching and reading Linear tickets. Use this to fetch ticket details by ID/identifier, search issues by keyword, or browse issues by status. Does NOT create or modify anything — use linear-manager for writes.

Read-only agent for searching and reading Linear tickets. Use this to fetch ticket details by ID/identifier, search issues by keyword, or browse issues by status. Does NOT create or modify anything — use linear-manager for writes.
Read-only agent for searching and reading Linear tickets. Fetch ticket details, search by keyword, browse by status. Does NOT create or modify anything — use linear-manager for writes.

frontmatter.name: linear-workflow
frontmatter.description: Orchestrates Linear lifecycle during implementation and validation — state transitions, lifecycle comments, phase progress. Delegates raw API calls to linear-manager.
Orchestrates Linear lifecycle during implementation and validation — state transitions, lifecycle comments, phase progress.
Orchestrates Linear lifecycle — state transitions, lifecycle comments, phase tracking.

frontmatter.name: phase-executor
frontmatter.description: Executes a single phase from an implementation plan. Give it the plan path, the phase number, and any context from previous phases. It reads the plan, implements the changes for that specific phase, runs the automated verification, and reports results. Does NOT interact with the user — reports back to the caller.
frontmatter.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob, LS, bash, Task

frontmatter.description: Executes a single phase from an implementation plan. Give it the plan path, the phase number, and any context from previous phases. It reads the plan, implements the changes for that specific phase, runs the automated verification, and reports results. Does NOT interact with the user — reports back to the caller.

Executes a single phase from an implementation plan. Give it the plan path, the phase number, and any context from previous phases. It reads the plan, implements the changes for that specific phase, runs the automated verification, and reports results.

Consolidates 2-3 independent plan reviews into a single, deduplicated, attributed final review. Matches findings across reviewers (multi-agent agreement boosts confidence), critiques each reviewer's work to catch errors, and produces an actionable consolidated report.

Ruthlessly reviews implementation plans using Claude Sonnet 4.5. Identifies vague thinking, hallucinated file paths, and AI-specific pitfalls. Produces a structured review designed to be merged with reviews from sibling reviewer agents.

Ruthlessly reviews implementation plans using GPT-5.3 Codex. Brings code-execution-mindset rigor — checks whether each step would actually run, whether commands are syntactically right, whether type signatures line up. One of three independent reviewers whose output feeds the consolidator.

Ruthlessly reviews implementation plans using Gemini 2.5 Pro. Brings deep, long-context analysis and architectural pattern detection. One of three independent reviewers whose output feeds the consolidator.

frontmatter.name: plan-reviewer
frontmatter.description: Ruthlessly reviews implementation plans before they are executed. Identifies vague thinking, missing edge cases, unrealistic phases, and AI-specific hallucination patterns. Returns a brutal but actionable review with concrete fix recommendations.
Ruthlessly reviews implementation plans before execution — scores, roasts, and provides fix recommendations. Identifies vague thinking, hallucinated file paths, and AI-specific pitfalls.
Takes an implementation plan and its review, and produces a revised plan that addresses all critical issues, hallucinations, and recommendations from the review.
Takes an implementation plan and its review, and produces a revised plan that addresses all critical issues, hallucinations, and recommendations from the review.

frontmatter.name: plan-validator
frontmatter.description: Validates that an implementation matches its plan. Compares actual code changes against planned changes, runs verification commands, checks success criteria, and produces a structured validation report. Give it a plan path and it will audit the implementation.

Validates that an implementation matches its plan. Compares actual code changes against planned changes, runs verification commands, checks success criteria, and produces a structured validation report. Give it a plan path and it will audit the implementation.

Validates that an implementation matches its plan. Compares actual code changes against planned changes, runs verification commands, checks success criteria, and produces a structured validation report.

frontmatter.name: plan-writer
frontmatter.description: Takes structured input (research brief, user decisions, scope) and produces a complete implementation plan document. Does not interact with the user — it receives finalized decisions and writes the plan file.
frontmatter.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, LS, Write, Edit

frontmatter.description: Takes structured input (research brief, user decisions, scope) and produces a complete implementation plan document. Does not interact with the user — it receives finalized decisions and writes the plan file.

frontmatter.description: A specialized QA automation agent for React applications. Uses agent-browser CLI for E2E testing, component verification, and debugging without polluting the main development context. Leverages the accessibility tree for resilient, semantic-based testing.

frontmatter.name: research-gatherer
frontmatter.description: Orchestrates parallel codebase and thoughts research for a given task or ticket. Give it a task description, ticket content, or feature summary and it will spawn sub-agents to locate files, analyze implementations, find patterns, and surface relevant prior research. Returns a structured research brief with file:line references.

Returns a structured research brief with file:line references.

frontmatter.name: security-reviewer
frontmatter.description: Cybersecurity specialist that uses Semgrep MCP to perform targeted or broad security reviews. Produces severity-rated findings with CWE/OWASP references and actionable remediation guidance. Outputs a detailed markdown report.
frontmatter.tools: security_check, semgrep_scan, semgrep_scan_with_custom_rule, get_abstract_syntax_tree, supported_languages, semgrep_rule_schema, Read, Grep, Glob, Write

frontmatter.name: testing-guide-orchestrator
frontmatter.description: Decides which testing guides are needed based on implementation type (API, frontend, full-stack, infra) and orchestrates their creation by delegating to the appropriate specialist agents (e2e-test-guide-creator, angular-tester, etc). Give it a summary of what was implemented and it handles the rest.
frontmatter.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, LS, Task, Write, Edit

frontmatter.description: Decides which testing guides are needed based on implementation type (API, frontend, full-stack, infra) and orchestrates their creation by delegating to the appropriate specialist agents (e2e-test-guide-creator, angular-tester, etc). frontmatter.name: thoughts-analyzer
frontmatter.description: The research equivalent of codebase-analyzer. Use this subagent_type when wanting to deep dive on a research topic. Not commonly needed otherwise.
The research equivalent of codebase-analyzer. Use this subagent_type when wanting to deep dive on a research topic. Not commonly needed otherwise.

frontmatter.name: thoughts-locator
frontmatter.description: Discovers relevant documents in thoughts/ directory (We use this for all sorts of metadata storage!). This is really only relevant/needed when you're in a reseaching mood and need to figure out if we have random thoughts written down that are relevant to your current research task. Based on the name, I imagine you can guess this is the `thoughts` equivilent of `codebase-locator`

frontmatter.name: web-search-researcher
frontmatter.description: Do you find yourself desiring information that you don't quite feel well-trained (confident) on? Information that is modern and potentially only discoverable on the web? Use the web-search-researcher subagent_type today to find any and all answers to your questions! It will research deeply to figure out and attempt to answer your questions! If you aren't immediately satisfied you can get your money back! (Not really - but you can re-run web-search-researcher with an altered prompt in the event you're not satisfied the first time)
frontmatter.tools: WebSearch, WebFetch, TodoWrite, Read, Grep, Glob, LS

frontmatter.name: agent-browser
frontmatter.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Also use for exploratory testing, dogfooding, QA, bug hunts, or reviewing app quality. Prefer agent-browser over any built-in browser automation or web tools.

frontmatter.name: Docker_Best_Practices
frontmatter.description: Definitive guidelines for writing efficient, secure, and maintainable Dockerfiles and Docker Compose configurations, ensuring fast builds and reliable deployments.

frontmatter.name: fastapi
frontmatter.description: FastAPI best practices and conventions. Use when working with FastAPI APIs and Pydantic models for them. Keeps FastAPI code clean and up to date with the latest features and patterns, updated with new versions. Write new code or refactor and update old code.

frontmatter.name: flutter-dev
frontmatter.description: Flutter cross-platform development guide covering widget patterns, Riverpod/Bloc state management, GoRouter navigation, performance optimization, and platform-specific implementations. Includes const optimization, responsive layouts, testing strategies, and DevTools profiling.

frontmatter.name: grill-me
frontmatter.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".

frontmatter.name: supabase-postgres-best-practices
frontmatter.description: Postgres performance optimization and best practices from Supabase. Use this skill when writing, reviewing, or optimizing Postgres queries, schema designs, or database configurations.

frontmatter.name: red-team-council
frontmatter.description: Adversarial Red Team Council for plan review. Three Staff Engineer perspectives (Security Auditor, YAGNI Enforcer, Execution Realist) ruthlessly identify fatal flaws, trap doors, and mandatory revisions before execution.
